NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus—by injunction of God our Saviour and Christ Jesus our hope,—
text.KJV: Paul an Apostle of Iesus Christ by the commaundement of God our Sauiour, & Lord Iesus Christ which is our hope,
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by command of God our savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
text.DRB: Paul an Apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Saviour, and of Christ Jesus our hope:

text.NRSV: To Timothy, my loyal child in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: unto Timothy, my true child in faith: favour, mercy, peace, from God our Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: Unto Timothie my own sonne in the Faith: Grace, mercie, and peace from God our Father, and Iesus Christ our Lord.
text.NABRE: to Timothy, my true child in faith: grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.DRB: To Timothee his beloved son in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father, and from Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I urge you, as I did when I was on my way to Macedonia, to remain in Ephesus so that you may instruct certain people not to teach any different doctrine,
text.Rotherham: Even as I exhorted thee to remain in Ephesus, when I was journeying into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some—
text.KJV: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,
text.NABRE: II. Sound Teaching Warning Against False Doctrine. I repeat the request I made of you when I was on my way to Macedonia, that you stay in Ephesus to instruct certain people not to teach false doctrines
text.DRB: As I desired thee to remain at Ephesus when I went into Macedonia, that thou shouldest denounce to certain not to teach otherwise,

text.NRSV: and not to occupy themselves with myths and endless genealogies that promote speculations rather than the divine training that is known by faith.
text.Rotherham: Not to be teaching otherwise, nor yet to be giving heed to stories and endless genealogies,—the which, bring, arguings, rather than that stewardship of God which is with faith;—
text.KJV: Neither giue heed to fables, and endlesse genealogies, which minister questions, rather then edifying which is in faith: so doe.
text.NABRE: or to concern themselves with myths and endless genealogies, which promote speculations rather than the plan of God that is to be received by faith.
text.DRB: nor to attend to fables and genealogies having no end: which minister questions rather than the edifying of God which is in faith.

text.NRSV: But the aim of such instruction is love that comes from a pure heart, a good conscience, and sincere faith.
text.Rotherham: Now, the end of the charge, is love—out of a pure heart, and a good conscience, and faith unfeigned,—
text.KJV: Now the end of the commandement is charity, out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith vnfained.
text.NABRE: The aim of this instruction is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
text.DRB: But the end of the precept is charity from a pure heart, and a good conscience, and a faith not feigned.

text.NRSV: Some people have deviated from these and turned to meaningless talk,
text.Rotherham: Which some, missing, have turned them aside unto idle talk,
text.KJV: From which some hauing swarued, haue turned aside vnto vaine iangling,
text.NABRE: Some people have deviated from these and turned to meaningless talk,
text.DRB: From the which things certain straying, are turned into vain-talk,

text.NRSV: desiring to be teachers of the law, without understanding either what they are saying or the things about which they make confident assertions.
text.Rotherham: Desiring to be law-teachers,—not understanding, either what they say or whereof they confidently affirm.
text.KJV: Desiring to bee teachers of the Law, vnderstāding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirme.
text.NABRE: wanting to be teachers of the law, but without understanding either what they are saying or what they assert with such assurance.
text.DRB: desirous to be doctors of the Law, not understanding neither what things they speak, nor of what they affirm.

text.NRSV: Now we know that the law is good, if one uses it legitimately.
text.Rotherham: Now we know that, excellent, is the law, if one put it to a lawful use:—
text.KJV: But we know that the Law is good, if a man vse it lawfully.
text.NABRE: We know that the law is good, provided that one uses it as law,
text.DRB: But we know that the Law is good, if a man use it lawfully:

text.NRSV: This means understanding that the law is laid down not for the innocent but for the lawless and disobedient, for the godless and sinful, for the unholy and profane, for those who kill their father or mother, for murderers,
text.Rotherham: Knowing this—that, to a righteous man, law, doth not apply, but to the lawless and insubordinate, ungodly and sinful, irreligious and profane, smiters of fathers and smiters of mothers, murderers,
text.KJV: Knowing this, that the Law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawlesse and disobedient, for the vngodly, and for sinners, for vnholy, and profane, for murderers of fathers, and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,
text.NABRE: with the understanding that law is meant not for a righteous person but for the lawless and unruly, the godless and sinful, the unholy and profane, those who kill their fathers or mothers, murderers,
text.DRB: knowing this, that the Law is not made to the just man, but to the unjust, & disobedient, to the impious & sinners, to the wicked & contaminate, to killers of fathers & killers of mothers, to murderers,

text.NRSV: fornicators, sodomites, slave traders, liars, perjurers, and whatever else is contrary to the sound teaching
text.Rotherham: fornicators, sodomites, man-stealers, liars, false- swearers,—and, if anything else, unto the healthful teaching, is opposed;—
text.KJV: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselues with mankinde, for men-stealers, for liars, for periured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine,
text.NABRE: the unchaste, sodomites, kidnappers, liars, perjurers, and whatever else is opposed to sound teaching,
text.DRB: to fornicators, to liers with mankind, to man-stealers, to liars, to perjured persons, and what other thing soever is contrary to sound doctrine,

text.NRSV: that conforms to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which he entrusted to me.
text.Rotherham: According to the glad-message of the glory of the happy God, with which entrusted am, I.
text.KJV: According to the glorious Gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.
text.NABRE: according to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, with which I have been entrusted. Gratitude for God’s Mercy.
text.DRB: which is according to the Gospel of the glory of the blessed God, which is committed to me.

text.NRSV: I am grateful to Christ Jesus our Lord, who has strengthened me, because he judged me faithful and appointed me to his service,
text.Rotherham: Grateful, am I unto him that empowered me, Christ Jesus our Lord, in that, faithful, he accounted me, putting me into ministry,—
text.KJV: And I thanke Christ Iesus our Lord, who hath enabled mee: for that he counted me faithfull, putting me into the Ministerie,
text.NABRE: I am grateful to him who has strengthened me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he considered me trustworthy in appointing me to the ministry.
text.DRB: I give him thanks which hath strengthened me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he hath esteemed me faithful, putting me in the ministry.

text.NRSV: even though I was formerly a blasphemer, a persecutor, and a man of violence. But I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief,
text.Rotherham: Though, formerly, a defamer, and persecutor, and insulter; nevertheless mercy was shown me, because, without knowledge, I acted, in unbelief:
text.KJV: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecuter, and iniurious. But I obtained mercie, because I did it ignorantly, in vnbeliefe.
text.NABRE: I was once a blasphemer and a persecutor and an arrogant man, but I have been mercifully treated because I acted out of ignorance in my unbelief.
text.DRB: Who before was blasphemous and a persecutor and contumelious. But I obtained the mercy of God, because I did it being ignorant in incredulity.

text.NRSV: and the grace of our Lord overflowed for me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Yet exceeding abundant was the favour of our Lord, with faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant, with faith, & loue, which is in Christ Iesus.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the grace of our Lord has been abundant, along with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: And the grace of our Lord over-abounded with faith and love, which is in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—of whom I am the foremost.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! and, of all acceptance, worthy,—that, Christ Jesus, came into the world, sinners, to save: of whom, the chief, am, I;—
text.KJV: This is a faithfull saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Iesus came into the world to saue sinners, of whom I am chiefe.
text.NABRE: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners. Of these I am the foremost.
text.DRB: A faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into this world to save sinners, of whom I am the chief.

text.NRSV: But for that very reason I received mercy, so that in me, as the foremost, Jesus Christ might display the utmost patience, making me an example to those who would come to believe in him for eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, on this account, was mercy shewn me,—that, in me, the chief, Christ Jesus might shew forth his entire longsuffering, for an ensample of them about to believe on him unto life age-abiding.
text.KJV: Howbeit, for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first, Iesus Christ might shew foorth all long suffering, for a paterne to them which should hereafter beleeue on him to life euerlasting.
text.NABRE: But for that reason I was mercifully treated, so that in me, as the foremost, Christ Jesus might display all his patience as an example for those who would come to believe in him for everlasting life.
text.DRB: But therefore have I obtained mercy: that in me first of all Christ Jesus might shew all patience, to the information of them that shall believe on him unto life everlasting.

text.NRSV: To the King of the ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto the King of the ages,—incorruptible, invisible, alone God, be honour and glory, unto the ages of ages, Amen!
text.KJV: Now vnto þ king eternal, immortall, inuisible, the onely wise God, be honour and glory for euer & euer. Amen.
text.NABRE: To the king of ages, incorruptible, invisible, the only God, honor and glory forever and ever. Amen. Responsibility of Timothy.
text.DRB: And to the King of the worlds, immortal, invisible, only God, honour & glory for ever and ever. Amen.

text.NRSV: I am giving you these instructions, Timothy, my child, in accordance with the prophecies made earlier concerning you, so that by following them you may fight the good fight,
text.Rotherham: This charge, I commit unto thee, child Timothy, According to the prophecies, running before on thee, in order that thou mightest war, with them, the noble warfare.
text.KJV: This charge I commit vnto thee, sonne Timothie, according to the prophesies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest warre a good warfare,
text.NABRE: I entrust this charge to you, Timothy, my child, in accordance with the prophetic words once spoken about you. Through them may you fight a good fight
text.DRB: This precept I commend to thee, o Timothee: according to the prophecies going before on thee, that thou war in them a good warfare,

text.NRSV: having faith and a good conscience. By rejecting conscience, certain persons have suffered shipwreck in the faith;
text.Rotherham: Holding faith and a good conscience,—which some, thrusting from them, concerning their faith, have made shipwreck:—
text.KJV: Holding faith, and a good conscience, which some hauing put away, concerning faith, haue made shipwracke.
text.NABRE: by having faith and a good conscience. Some, by rejecting conscience, have made a shipwreck of their faith,
text.DRB: having faith and a good conscience, which certain repelling have made shipwreck about the faith.

text.NRSV: among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have turned over to Satan, so that they may learn not to blaspheme.
text.Rotherham: Of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may be taught by discipline not to be defaming.
text.KJV: Of whom is Hymeneus and Alexander, whome I haue deliuered vnto Satan, that they may learne not to blaspheme.
text.NABRE: among them Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have handed over to Satan to be taught not to blaspheme.
text.DRB: Of whom is Hymenæus & Alexander: whom I have delivered to Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.

text.NRSV: First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for everyone,
text.Rotherham: I exhort, therefore, first of all, that there be made—supplications, prayers, intercessions, thankgivings, in behalf of all men,
text.KJV: I exhort therefore, that first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giuing of thanks be made for all men:
text.NABRE: III. Problems of Discipline Chapter 2 Prayer and Conduct. First of all, then, I ask that supplications, prayers, petitions, and thanksgivings be offered for everyone,
text.DRB: I desire therefore first of all things that obsecrations, prayers, postulations, thanks-givings be made for all men,

text.NRSV: for kings and all who are in high positions, so that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and dignity.
text.Rotherham: In behalf of kings, and all them who are, in eminent station; in order that, an undisturbed, and quiet life, we may lead, in all godliness and gravity:
text.KJV: For Kings, and for all that are in authoritie, that we may leade a quiet and peaceable life in all godlinesse and honestie.
text.NABRE: for kings and for all in authority, that we may lead a quiet and tranquil life in all devotion and dignity.
text.DRB: for kings and all that are in preeminence: that we may lead a quiet and a peaceable life in all piety and chastity.

text.NRSV: This is right and is acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
text.Rotherham: This, is comely and acceptable before our Saviour God,
text.KJV: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Sauiour,
text.NABRE: This is good and pleasing to God our savior,
text.DRB: For this is good and acceptable before our Saviour God,

text.NRSV: who desires everyone to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
text.Rotherham: Who willeth, all men, to be saved, and, unto a personal knowledge of truth, to come;
text.KJV: Who will haue all men to bee saued, and to come vnto the knowledge of the trueth.
text.NABRE: who wills everyone to be saved and to come to knowledge of the truth.
text.DRB: who will all men to be saved, and to come to the knowledge of the truth.

text.NRSV: For there is one God; there is also one mediator between God and humankind, Christ Jesus, himself human,
text.Rotherham: For there is, one, God, one, mediator also, between God and men,—a man—Christ Jesus:
text.KJV: For there is one God, and one Mediatour betweene God and men, the man Christ Iesus,
text.NABRE: For there is one God. There is also one mediator between God and the human race, Christ Jesus, himself human,
text.DRB: For there is one God, one also mediator of God and men, man Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: who gave himself a ransom for all—this was attested at the right time.
text.Rotherham: Who gave himself a ransom in behalf of all,—the testimony, in its own fit times:
text.KJV: Who gaue himselfe a ransome for all, to be testified in due time.
text.NABRE: who gave himself as ransom for all. This was the testimony at the proper time.
text.DRB: who gave himself a redemption for all, whose testimony in due times is confirmed.

text.NRSV: For this I was appointed a herald and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
text.Rotherham: Unto which, I, have been appointed proclaimer and apostle—Truth I speak, I utter no falsehood—a teacher of nations, in faith and truth.
text.KJV: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an Apostle (I speake the trueth in Christ, and lie not) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and veritie.
text.NABRE: For this I was appointed preacher and apostle (I am speaking the truth, I am not lying), teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
text.DRB: Wherein I am appointed a preacher and an Apostle (I say the truth, I lie not) doctor of the Gentiles in faith and truth.

text.NRSV: I desire, then, that in every place the men should pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or argument;
text.Rotherham: I am minded, therefore, that—the men in every place be offering prayer, uplifting hands of lovingkindness, apart from anger and disputings;
text.KJV: I will therefore that men pray euery where, lifting vp holy handes without wrath, and doubting.
text.NABRE: It is my wish, then, that in every place the men should pray, lifting up holy hands, without anger or argument.
text.DRB: I will therefore that men pray in every place: lifting up pure hands, without anger and altercation.

text.NRSV: also that the women should dress themselves modestly and decently in suitable clothing, not with hair braided with gold or pearls or expensive clothes,
text.Rotherham: In the same way, that, the women, in seemly attire, with modesty and sober-mindedness, be adorning themselves,—not with plaitings and ornamentation of gold, or with pearls, or with costly apparel,—
text.KJV: In like maner also, that women adorne themselues in modest apparell, with shamefastnesse and sobrietie, not with broided haire, or gold, or pearles, or costly aray,
text.NABRE: Similarly, [too,] women should adorn themselves with proper conduct, with modesty and self-control, not with braided hairstyles and gold ornaments, or pearls, or expensive clothes,
text.DRB: In like manner women also in comely attire: with demureness and sobriety adorning themselves, not in plaited hair, or gold, or precious stones, or gorgeous apparel,

text.NRSV: but with good works, as is proper for women who profess reverence for God.
text.Rotherham: But, which becometh women promising godliness, through means of good works.
text.KJV: But (which becommeth women professing godlines) with good works.
text.NABRE: but rather, as befits women who profess reverence for God, with good deeds.
text.DRB: but that which becometh women professing piety by good works.

text.NRSV: Let a woman learn in silence with full submission.
text.Rotherham: Let, a woman, in quietness, be learning in all submission;
text.KJV: Let the woman learne in silence with all subiection:
text.NABRE: A woman must receive instruction silently and under complete control.
text.DRB: Let a woman learn in silence, with all subjection.

text.NRSV: I permit no woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she is to keep silent.
text.Rotherham: But, teaching—unto a woman, I do not permit, nor yet to have authority over a man,—but to be in quietness;
text.KJV: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to vsurpe authoritie ouer the man, but to be in silence.
text.NABRE: I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man. She must be quiet.
text.DRB: But to teach I permit not unto a woman, nor to have dominion over the man: but to be in silence.

text.NRSV: For Adam was formed first, then Eve;
text.Rotherham: For, Adam, first was formed, then Eve,
text.KJV: For Adam was first formed, then Eue:
text.NABRE: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.
text.DRB: For Adam was formed first; then Eve.

text.NRSV: and Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a transgressor.
text.Rotherham: And, Adam, was not deceived, whereas, the woman, having been wholly deceived, hath come to be, in transgression;
text.KJV: And Adam was not deceiued, but the woman being deceiued was in the transgression:
text.NABRE: Further, Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and transgressed.
text.DRB: And Adam was not seduced: but the woman being seduced, was in prevarication.

text.NRSV: Yet she will be saved through childbearing, provided they continue in faith and love and holiness, with modesty.
text.Rotherham: She shall be saved, however, through means of the child-bearing,—if they abide in faith, and love, and holiness, with sobermindedness. Faithful, the saying.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding she shall be saued in child-bearing, if they continue in faith and charitie, and holinesse, with sobrietie.
text.NABRE: But she will be saved through motherhood, provided women persevere in faith and love and holiness, with self-control.
text.DRB: Yet she shall be saved by generation of children: if they continue in faith and love and sanctification with sobriety.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure: whoever aspires to the office of bishop desires a noble task.
text.Rotherham: If anyone, for oversight, is eager, a noble work, doth he covet:—
text.KJV: This is a true saying: If a man desire the office of a Bishop, he desireth a good worke.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Qualifications of Various Ministers. This saying is trustworthy: whoever aspires to the office of bishop desires a noble task.
text.DRB: Afaithful saying. If a man desire a Bishop's office, he desireth a good work.

text.NRSV: Now a bishop must be above reproach, married only once, temperate, sensible, respectable, hospitable, an apt teacher,
text.Rotherham: It is needful, then, for, the overseer, to be irreproachable, a husband, of one wife, sober, of sound mind, orderly, hospitable, apt in teaching,
text.KJV: A Bishop then must be blamelesse, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behauiour, giuen to hospitalitie, apt to teach;
text.NABRE: Therefore, a bishop must be irreproachable, married only once, temperate, self-controlled, decent, hospitable, able to teach,
text.DRB: It behoveth therefore a Bishop to be irreprehensible, the husband of one wife, sober, wise, comely, chaste, a man of hospitality, a teacher,

text.NRSV: not a drunkard, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, and not a lover of money.
text.Rotherham: Not given to wine, not ready to wound, but considerate, averse to contention, not fond of money,
text.KJV: Not giuen to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre, but patient, not a brawler, not couetous;
text.NABRE: not a drunkard, not aggressive, but gentle, not contentious, not a lover of money.
text.DRB: not given to wine, no fighter, but modest, no quarreler, not covetous,

text.NRSV: He must manage his own household well, keeping his children submissive and respectful in every way—
text.Rotherham: Over his own house, presiding, well, having, children, in submission, with all dignity;
text.KJV: One that ruleth well his owne house, hauing his children in subiection with all grauitie.
text.NABRE: He must manage his own household well, keeping his children under control with perfect dignity;
text.DRB: well ruling his own house, having his children subject with all chastity.

text.NRSV: for if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how can he take care of God’s church?
text.Rotherham: Whereas, if anyone, over his own house, cannot, preside, how, of an assembly of God, shall he take care?
text.KJV: (For if a man know not how to rule his owne house, how shall he take care of the Church of God?)
text.NABRE: for if a man does not know how to manage his own household, how can he take care of the church of God?
text.DRB: But if a man know not to rule his own house, how shall he have care of the Church of God?

text.NRSV: He must not be a recent convert, or he may be puffed up with conceit and fall into the condemnation of the devil.
text.Rotherham: Not a new convert, lest, being beclouded, into the sentence of the adversary, he fall;
text.KJV: Not a nouice, lest being lifted vp with pride, hee fall into the condemnation of the deuill.
text.NABRE: He should not be a recent convert, so that he may not become conceited and thus incur the devil’s punishment.
text.DRB: Not a neophyte: lest puffed into pride, he fall into the judgement of the devil.

text.NRSV: Moreover, he must be well thought of by outsiders, so that he may not fall into disgrace and the snare of the devil.
text.Rotherham: It is needful, moreover, to have, an honourable testimony also, from them who are without, lest, into reproach, he fall, and the snare of the adversary.
text.KJV: Moreouer, hee must haue a good report of them which are without, lest he fall into reproch, and the snare of the deuill.
text.NABRE: He must also have a good reputation among outsiders, so that he may not fall into disgrace, the devil’s trap.
text.DRB: And he must have also good testimony of them that are without: that he fall not into reproach and the snare of the devil.

text.NRSV: Deacons likewise must be serious, not double-tongued, not indulging in much wine, not greedy for money;
text.Rotherham: Ministers, in the same way,—dignified, not double-tongued, not, to much wine, given, not greedy of base gain,
text.KJV: Likewise must the Deacons bee graue, not double tongued, not giuen to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre,
text.NABRE: Similarly, deacons must be dignified, not deceitful, not addicted to drink, not greedy for sordid gain,
text.DRB: Deacons in like manner chaste, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not followers of filthy lucre:

text.NRSV: they must hold fast to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
text.Rotherham: Holding the sacred secret of the faith in a pure conscience;
text.KJV: Holding the mysterie of the faith in a pure conscience.
text.NABRE: holding fast to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
text.DRB: having the mystery of faith in a pure conscience.

text.NRSV: And let them first be tested; then, if they prove themselves blameless, let them serve as deacons.
text.Rotherham: But let, these also, be proved first, then, let them be ministering, being, unaccusable:
text.KJV: And let these also first be proued; then let them vse the office of a Deacon, being found blamelesse.
text.NABRE: Moreover, they should be tested first; then, if there is nothing against them, let them serve as deacons.
text.DRB: And let these also be proved first: & so let them minister, having no crime.

text.NRSV: Women likewise must be serious, not slanderers, but temperate, faithful in all things.
text.Rotherham: Wives, in the same way,—dignified, not given to intrigue, sober, faithful in all things.
text.KJV: Euen so must their wiues be graue; not slanderers, sober, faithfull in all things.
text.NABRE: Women, similarly, should be dignified, not slanderers, but temperate and faithful in everything.
text.DRB: The women in like manner chaste, not detracting, sober, faithful in all things.

text.NRSV: Let deacons be married only once, and let them manage their children and their households well;
text.Rotherham: Let, ministers, be husbands of, one wife, over children, presiding, well, and over their own houses;
text.KJV: Let the Deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children, and their owne houses well.
text.NABRE: Deacons may be married only once and must manage their children and their households well.
text.DRB: Let deacons be the husbands of one wife: which rule well their children, and their houses.

text.NRSV: for those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: For, they who have ministered well, a good degree for themselves, are acquiring, and great freedom of speech in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: For they that haue vsed the office of a Deacon well, purchase to themselues a good degree, and great boldnesse in the faith, which is in Christ Iesus.
text.NABRE: Thus those who serve well as deacons gain good standing and much confidence in their faith in Christ Jesus. The Mystery of Our Religion.
text.DRB: For they that have ministered well, shall purchase to themselves a good degree, and much confidence in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these instructions to you so that,
text.Rotherham: These things, unto thee, I am writing, hoping to come [unto thee] shortly,—
text.KJV: These things write I vnto thee, hoping to come vnto thee shortly.
text.NABRE: I am writing you about these matters, although I hope to visit you soon.
text.DRB: These things I write to thee, hoping that I shall come to thee quickly.

text.NRSV: if I am delayed, you may know how one ought to behave in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and bulwark of the truth.
text.Rotherham: But, if I should tarry, that thou mayest know—how it behoveth, in a house of God, to behave oneself,—the which, is an assembly of a Living God, a pillar and basement of the truth;—
text.KJV: But if I tary long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behaue thy selfe in the House of God, which is the Church of the liuing God, the pillar and ground of the trueth.
text.NABRE: But if I should be delayed, you should know how to behave in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of truth.
text.DRB: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to converse in the house of God, which is the Church of the living God, the pillar and ground of truth.

text.NRSV: Without any doubt, the mystery of our religion is great: He was revealed in flesh, vindicated in spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among Gentiles, believed in in the world, taken up in glory.
text.Rotherham: And, confessedly great, is the sacred secret of godliness,—Who was made manifest in flesh, was declared righteous in spirit, was made visible unto messengers, was proclaimed among nations, was believed on in the world, was taken up in glory.
text.KJV: And without controuersie, great is the mysterie of godlinesse: God was manifest in the flesh, iustified in the Spirit, seene of Angels, preached vnto the Gentiles, beleeued on in the world, receiued vp into glory.
text.NABRE: Undeniably great is the mystery of devotion, Who was manifested in the flesh, vindicated in the spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed to the Gentiles, believed in throughout the world, taken up in glory.
text.DRB: And manifestly it is a great sacrament of piety, which was manifested in flesh, was justified in spirit, appeared to Angels, hath been preached to Gentiles, is believed in the world, is assumpted in glory.

text.NRSV: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will renounce the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the Spirit, expressly saith—that, in later seasons, some will revolt from the faith, giving heed unto seducing spirits, and unto teachings of demons—
text.KJV: Now the Spirit speaketh expresly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giuing heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of deuils:
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 False Asceticism. Now the Spirit explicitly says that in the last times some will turn away from the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and demonic instructions
text.DRB: And the Spirit manifestly saith that in the last times certain shall depart from the faith attending to spirits of error, and doctrines of devils,

text.NRSV: through the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared with a hot iron.
text.Rotherham: in hypocrisy, speaking falsehood, [of demons] cauterised in their own conscience,—
text.KJV: Speaking lies in hypocrisie, hauing their conscience seared with a hote iron,
text.NABRE: through the hypocrisy of liars with branded consciences.
text.DRB: speaking lies in hypocrisy, and having their conscience seared,

text.NRSV: They forbid marriage and demand abstinence from foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
text.Rotherham: Forbidding to marry, [commanding] to abstain from foods which, God, created to be received with thanksgiving by them who believe and personally know the truth;
text.KJV: Forbidding to marry, and commanding to absteine from meates, which God hath created to bee receiued with thankesgiuing of them which beleeue, and know the trueth.
text.NABRE: They forbid marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
text.DRB: forbidding to marry, to abstain from meats which God created to receive with thanks-giving for the faithful, and them that have known the truth.

text.NRSV: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, provided it is received with thanksgiving;
text.Rotherham: Because, every creature of God, is good, and nothing to be cast away, if, with thanksgiving, it be received,—
text.KJV: For euery creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be receiued with thankesgiuing:
text.NABRE: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected when received with thanksgiving,
text.DRB: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be rejected that is received with thanks-giving.

text.NRSV: for it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
text.Rotherham: For it is hallowed by the word of God and intercession,
text.KJV: For it is sanctified by the word of God, and prayer.
text.NABRE: for it is made holy by the invocation of God in prayer.
text.DRB: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

text.NRSV: If you put these instructions before the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound teaching that you have followed.
text.Rotherham: These things, submitting to the brethren, thou shall be, a noble, minister of Christ Jesus, nourishing thyself with the words of the faith, and of the noble teaching which thou hast closely studied.
text.KJV: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Iesus Christ, nourished vp in the wordes of faith, and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.
text.NABRE: Counsel to Timothy. If you will give these instructions to the brothers, you will be a good minister of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound teaching you have followed.
text.DRB: These things proposing to the brethren, thou shalt be a good minister of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith and the good doctrine which thou hast attained unto.

text.NRSV: Have nothing to do with profane myths and old wives’ tales. Train yourself in godliness,
text.Rotherham: But, from the profane and old-wives’ stories, excuse thyself, and be training thyself unto godliness;
text.KJV: But refuse prophane and olde wiues fables, and exercise thy selfe rather vnto godlinesse.
text.NABRE: Avoid profane and silly myths. Train yourself for devotion,
text.DRB: But foolish and old wives' fables avoid: and exercise thyself to piety.

text.NRSV: for, while physical training is of some value, godliness is valuable in every way, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.
text.Rotherham: For, the bodily training, for little, is profitable, whereas, godliness, for all things is profitable,—having, promise, of life—the present and the coming.
text.KJV: For bodily exercise profiteth litle, but godlinesse is profitable vnto all things, hauing promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
text.NABRE: for, while physical training is of limited value, devotion is valuable in every respect, since it holds a promise of life both for the present and for the future.
text.DRB: For corporal exercise is profitable to little: but piety is profitable to all things: having promise of the life that now is, and of that to come.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! and, of all acceptance, worthy;
text.KJV: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation:
text.NABRE: This saying is trustworthy and deserves full acceptance.
text.DRB: Afaithful saying and worthy of all acceptation:

text.NRSV: For to this end we toil and struggle, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
text.Rotherham: For, to this end, are we toiling and contending, because we have set our hope on a Living God, Who is Saviour of all men—specially, of such as believe.
text.KJV: For therfore we both labour, and suffer reproch, because we trust in the liuing God, who is the Sauiour of all men, specially of those that beleeue.
text.NABRE: For this we toil and struggle, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the savior of all, especially of those who believe.
text.DRB: For to this purpose we labour and are reviled, because we hope in the living God which is the Saviour of all men, especially of the faithful.

text.NRSV: These are the things you must insist on and teach.
text.Rotherham: Be giving these things in charge, and be teaching:—
text.KJV: These things command & teach.
text.NABRE: Command and teach these things.
text.DRB: Command these things and teach.

text.NRSV: Let no one despise your youth, but set the believers an example in speech and conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
text.Rotherham: Let, no one, despise, thy youth, but, an ensample, become thou of the faithful,—in discourse, in behaviour, in love, in faith, in chastity.
text.KJV: Let no man despise thy youth, but be thou an example of the beleeuers, in word, in conuersation, in charitie, in spirit, in faith, in puritie.
text.NABRE: Let no one have contempt for your youth, but set an example for those who believe, in speech, conduct, love, faith, and purity.
text.DRB: Let no man contemn thy youth: but be an example of the faithful, in word, in conversation, in charity, in faith, in chastity.

text.NRSV: Until I arrive, give attention to the public reading of scripture, to exhorting, to teaching.
text.Rotherham: While I am coming, be giving heed—to the reading, to the exhorting, to the teaching:
text.KJV: Till I come, giue attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.
text.NABRE: Until I arrive, attend to the reading, exhortation, and teaching.
text.DRB: Till I come, attend unto reading, exhortation, doctrine.

text.NRSV: Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you through prophecy with the laying on of hands by the council of elders.
text.Rotherham: Be not careless of the gift of favour, that is in thee, which was given thee through means of prophesying, along with a laying on of the hands of the eldership.
text.KJV: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was giuen thee by prophesie, with the laying on of the hands of the Presbyterie.
text.NABRE: Do not neglect the gift you have, which was conferred on you through the prophetic word with the imposition of hands of the presbyterate.
text.DRB: Neglect not the grace that is in thee: which is given thee by prophecy, with imposition of the hands of priesthood.

text.NRSV: Put these things into practice, devote yourself to them, so that all may see your progress.
text.Rotherham: These things, be thy care, in these things, be thou, that, thine advancement, may be, manifest, unto all:
text.KJV: Meditate vpon these things, giue thy selfe wholly to them, that thy profiting may appeare to all.
text.NABRE: Be diligent in these matters, be absorbed in them, so that your progress may be evident to everyone.
text.DRB: These things do thou mediate, be in these things: that thy profiting may be manifest to all.

text.NRSV: Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; continue in these things, for in doing so you will save both yourself and your hearers.
text.Rotherham: Be giving heed to thyself, and to thy teaching,—abide still in them; for, this doing, both, thyself, shalt thou save, and them that hearken to thee.
text.KJV: Take heed vnto thy selfe, and vnto the doctrine: continue in them: for in doing this, thou shalt both saue thy selfe, and them that heare thee.
text.NABRE: Attend to yourself and to your teaching; persevere in both tasks, for by doing so you will save both yourself and those who listen to you.
text.DRB: Attend to thyself, and to doctrine: be earnest in them. For, this doing, thou shalt save both thyself and them that hear thee.

text.NRSV: Do not speak harshly to an older man, but speak to him as to a father, to younger men as brothers,
text.Rotherham: An elderly man, do not thou reprimand, but beseech him, as [though he were thy] father,—younger men, as brothers,
text.KJV: Rebuke not an Elder, but intreate him as a father, and the yonger men as brethren:
text.NABRE: IV. Duties Toward Others Chapter 5 Do not rebuke an older man, but appeal to him as a father. Treat younger men as brothers,
text.DRB: A Senior rebuke not: but beseech as a father: young men, as brethren:

text.NRSV: to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters—with absolute purity.
text.Rotherham: Elderly women, as mothers, younger women, as sisters, in all chastity.
text.KJV: The elder women as mothers, the yonger as sisters with all puritie.
text.NABRE: older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters with complete purity.
text.DRB: old women, as mothers: young women, as sisters, in all chastity.

text.NRSV: Honor widows who are really widows.
text.Rotherham: Widows, honour thou—who indeed are widows:—
text.KJV: Honour widowes that are widowes indeed.
text.NABRE: Rules for Widows. Honor widows who are truly widows.
text.DRB: Honour widows, which are widows indeed.

text.NRSV: If a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn their religious duty to their own family and make some repayment to their parents; for this is pleasing in God’s sight.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, if, any widow, hath, children or grandchildren, let them be learning—first, unto their own house, to be shewing reverence, and, returns, to be making unto their progenitors; for, this, is acceptable before God;
text.KJV: But if any widow haue children or nephewes, let them learne first to shew pietie at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.
text.NABRE: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, let these first learn to perform their religious duty to their own family and to make recompense to their parents, for this is pleasing to God.
text.DRB: But if any widow have children or nephews; let her learn first to rule her own house, & to render mutual duty to her parents. For this is acceptable before God.

text.NRSV: The real widow, left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day;
text.Rotherham: But, she who is indeed a widow, and is left alone, hath turned her hope towards God, and is giving attendance unto the supplications and the prayers, night and day,—
text.KJV: Now she that is a widow in deed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.
text.NABRE: The real widow, who is all alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.
text.DRB: But she that is a widow indeed and desolate, let her hope in God, and continue in obsecrations & prayers night and day.

text.NRSV: but the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, she that runneth riot, while living, is, dead:
text.KJV: But she that liueth in pleasure, is dead while she liueth.
text.NABRE: But the one who is self-indulgent is dead while she lives.
text.DRB: For she that is in deliciousness, living is dead.

text.NRSV: Give these instructions as well, so that they may be above reproach.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, be giving in charge, that, without reproach, they may be;
text.KJV: And these things giue in charge, that they may be blamelesse.
text.NABRE: Command this, so that they may be irreproachable.
text.DRB: And this command that they be blameless.

text.NRSV: And whoever does not provide for relatives, and especially for family members, has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
text.Rotherham: If, however, anyone, for his own, and specially them of his household, taketh not forethought, his faith, hath he denied, and is worse, than one without faith!
text.KJV: But if any prouide not for his owne, & specially for those of his owne house, hee hath denied the faith, and is worse then an infidel.
text.NABRE: And whoever does not provide for relatives and especially family members has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
text.DRB: But if any man have not care of his own, and especially of his domesticals, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.

text.NRSV: Let a widow be put on the list if she is not less than sixty years old and has been married only once;
text.Rotherham: Let, a widow, be put on the list—having become, not less than sixty years old, one man’s, wife,
text.KJV: Let not a widow bee taken into the number, vnder threescore yeeres old, hauing bene the wife of one man,
text.NABRE: Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years old, married only once,
text.DRB: Let a widow be chosen of no less than three-score years, which hath been the wife of one husband,

text.NRSV: she must be well attested for her good works, as one who has brought up children, shown hospitality, washed the saints’ feet, helped the afflicted, and devoted herself to every good work.
text.Rotherham: In noble works, being well-attested;—if she hath nourished children, if she hath shewn hospitality, if, saints feet, she hath washed, if, them who were in tribulation, she hath succoured, if, in every good, work she hath followed on:
text.KJV: Well reported of for good works, if shee haue brought vp children, if shee haue lodged strangers, if she haue washed the Saints feet, if shee haue releeued the afflicted, if shee haue diligently followed euery good worke.
text.NABRE: with a reputation for good works, namely, that she has raised children, practiced hospitality, washed the feet of the holy ones, helped those in distress, involved herself in every good work.
text.DRB: having testimony in good works, if she have brought up her children, if she have received to harbour, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have ministered to them that suffer tribulation, if she have followed every good work.

text.NRSV: But refuse to put younger widows on the list; for when their sensual desires alienate them from Christ, they want to marry,
text.Rotherham: But, younger widows, decline thou; for, as soon as they wax wanton against the Christ, to marry, are they determined,—
text.KJV: But the yonger widowes refuse: for when they haue begunne to waxe wanton against Christ, they will marry,
text.NABRE: But exclude younger widows, for when their sensuality estranges them from Christ, they want to marry
text.DRB: But the younger widows avoid. For when they shall be wanton in Christ, they will marry:

text.NRSV: and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
text.Rotherham: Having, as sentence,—that, their first faith, they have set at nought;
text.KJV: Hauing damnation, because they haue cast off their first faith.
text.NABRE: and will incur condemnation for breaking their first pledge.
text.DRB: having damnation, because they have made void their first faith.

text.NRSV: Besides that, they learn to be idle, gadding about from house to house; and they are not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not say.
text.Rotherham: At the same time, to be idlers, are they learning, going about from house to house; and, not only idlers, but, gossips, also, and, busybodies,—saying the things they ought not,
text.KJV: And withall they learne to bee idle, wandering about from house to house; and not onely idle, but tatlers also, and busibodies, speaking things which they ought not.
text.NABRE: And furthermore, they learn to be idlers, going about from house to house, and not only idlers but gossips and busybodies as well, talking about things that ought not to be mentioned.
text.DRB: And withal idle also they learn to go about from house to house: not only idle, but also full of words & curious, speaking things which they ought not.

text.NRSV: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, and manage their households, so as to give the adversary no occasion to revile us.
text.Rotherham: I am minded, therefore, that, the younger ones, marry, bear children, be mistress of the house,—giving, no single occasion, unto the opposer, as a cause, of reviling;
text.KJV: I will therefore that the yonger women marry, beare children, guid the house, giue none occasion to the aduersary to speake reprochfully.
text.NABRE: So I would like younger widows to marry, have children, and manage a home, so as to give the adversary no pretext for maligning us.
text.DRB: I will therefore the younger to marry, to bring forth children, to be housewives: to give no occasion to the adversary for to speak evil.

text.NRSV: For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
text.Rotherham: For, already, some have turned aside after Satan:
text.KJV: For some are already turned aside after Satan.
text.NABRE: For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
text.DRB: For now certain are turned back after Satan.

text.NRSV: If any believing woman has relatives who are widows, let her assist them; let the church not be burdened, so that it can assist those who are real widows.
text.Rotherham: If any, believing woman, hath widows, let her be giving them succour, and not suffer the assembly to be burdened,—that, them who are indeed widows, it may itself succour.
text.KJV: If any man or woman that beleeueth haue widowes, let them relieue them, and let not the Church be charged, that it may relieue them that are widowes indeed.
text.NABRE: If any woman believer has widowed relatives, she must assist them; the church is not to be burdened, so that it will be able to help those who are truly widows. Rules for Presbyters.
text.DRB: If any faithful man have widows, let him minister to them, and let not the Church be burdened: that there may be sufficient for them that are widows indeed.

text.NRSV: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching;
text.Rotherham: Let, the well presiding elders, of double honour, be accounted worthy, especially they who toil in discourse and teaching;
text.KJV: Let the Elders that rule well, be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.
text.NABRE: Presbyters who preside well deserve double honor, especially those who toil in preaching and teaching.
text.DRB: The priests that rule well, let them be esteemed worthy of double honour: especially they that labour in the word and doctrine.

text.NRSV: for the scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The laborer deserves to be paid.”
text.Rotherham: For the scripture saith—A threshing ox, shalt thou not muzzle, and—Worthy, is the workman, of his hire.
text.KJV: For the Scripture saith, Thou shalt not mousell the oxe that treadeth out the corne: and, The labourer is worthy of his reward.
text.NABRE: For the scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox when it is threshing,” and, “A worker deserves his pay.”
text.DRB: For the Scripture saith: Thou shalt not muzzle the mouth to the ox that treadeth out the corn; and, The work-man is worthy of his hire.

text.NRSV: Never accept an accusation against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
text.Rotherham: Against an elder, entertain not, an accusation, except at, [the mouth of] two, or three, witnesses;
text.KJV: Against an Elder receiue not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.
text.NABRE: Do not accept an accusation against a presbyter unless it is supported by two or three witnesses.
text.DRB: Against a priest receive not accusation, but under two or three witnesses.

text.NRSV: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest also may stand in fear.
text.Rotherham: But, them who are sinning, before all, do thou reprove, that, the rest also, may have, fear:—
text.KJV: Them that sinne rebuke before all, that others also may feare.
text.NABRE: Reprimand publicly those who do sin, so that the rest also will be afraid.
text.DRB: Them that sin, reprove before all: that the rest also may have fear.

text.NRSV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels, I warn you to keep these instructions without prejudice, doing nothing on the basis of partiality.
text.Rotherham: I adjure thee, before God, and Christ Jesus, and the chosen messengers, that, these things, thou observe, apart from prejudgment, doing, nothing, by partiality.
text.KJV: I charge thee before God, and the Lord Iesus Christ, and the elect Angels, that thou obserue these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partialitie.
text.NABRE: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to keep these rules without prejudice, doing nothing out of favoritism.
text.DRB: I testify before God and Christ Jesus, and the elect Angels, that thou keep these things without prejudice, doing nothing by declining to the one part.

text.NRSV: Do not ordain anyone hastily, and do not participate in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
text.Rotherham: Hands, suddenly, upon no one, lay, neither have fellowship with sins of strangers: thyself, keep, chaste:—
text.KJV: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither bee partaker of other mens sinnes. Keepe thy selfe pure.
text.NABRE: Do not lay hands too readily on anyone, and do not share in another’s sins. Keep yourself pure.
text.DRB: Impose hands on no man lightly, neither do thou communicate with other men's sins. Keep thyself chaste.

text.NRSV: No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
text.Rotherham: No longer, be a water-drinker, but, of a little wine, make use, because of thy stomach and thy, frequent, sicknesses.
text.KJV: Drinke no longer water, but vse a little wine for thy stomackes sake, and thine often infirmities.
text.NABRE: Stop drinking only water, but have a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
text.DRB: Drink not yet water; but use a little wine for thy stomach, and thy often infirmities.

text.NRSV: The sins of some people are conspicuous and precede them to judgment, while the sins of others follow them there.
text.Rotherham: Some men’s sins, are, openly evident, leading on into judgment, with some, however, they even follow after;
text.KJV: Some mens sinnes are open before hand, going before to iudgement: and some men they follow after.
text.NABRE: Some people’s sins are public, preceding them to judgment; but other people are followed by their sins.
text.DRB: Certain men's sins be manifest, going before to judgement: and certain men they follow.

text.NRSV: So also good works are conspicuous; and even when they are not, they cannot remain hidden.
text.Rotherham: In the same way, the noble works also, are openly evident,—and, they that are otherwise, cannot be hid.
text.KJV: Likewise also the good works of some are manifest before hand, and they that are otherwise, cannot be hid.
text.NABRE: Similarly, good works are also public; and even those that are not cannot remain hidden.
text.DRB: In like manner also good deeds be manifest, and they that are otherwise, cannot be hid.

text.NRSV: Let all who are under the yoke of slavery regard their masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.
text.Rotherham: Let, as many as are servants under a yoke, be counting, their own masters, worthy, of all honour, lest, the name of God and the teaching, be defamed.
text.KJV: Let as many seruants as are vnder the yoke, count their owne masters worthy of all honour, that the Name of God, and his doctrine be not blasphemed.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Rules for Slaves. Those who are under the yoke of slavery must regard their masters as worthy of full respect, so that the name of God and our teaching may not suffer abuse.
text.DRB: Whosoever are servants under yoke, let them count their masters worthy of all honour; lest the name of our Lord and his doctrine be blasphemed.

text.NRSV: Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful to them on the ground that they are members of the church; rather they must serve them all the more, since those who benefit by their service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these duties.
text.Rotherham: They, however, that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are, brethren but, the more, be doing them service, because, believing and beloved, are they who, from the good workmanship, receive advantage. These things, be teaching, and exhorting:—
text.KJV: And they that haue beleeuing masters, let them not despise them because they are brethren: but rather doe them seruice, because they are faithfull and beloued, partakers of the benefite: These things teach and exhort.
text.NABRE: Those whose masters are believers must not take advantage of them because they are brothers but must give better service because those who will profit from their work are believers and are beloved. V. False Teaching and True Wealth Teach and urge these things.
text.DRB: But they that have faithful masters, let them not contemn them because they are brethren, but serve the rather, because they be faithful and beloved, which are partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.

text.NRSV: Whoever teaches otherwise and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that is in accordance with godliness
text.Rotherham: If anyone doth otherwise teach, and doth not adhere to healthful discourses—those of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the teaching that is, according to godliness,
text.KJV: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, euen the wordes of our Lord Iesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godlinesse:
text.NABRE: Whoever teaches something different and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the religious teaching
text.DRB: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to that doctrine which is according to piety,

text.NRSV: is conceited, understanding nothing, and has a morbid craving for controversy and for disputes about words. From these come envy, dissension, slander, base suspicions,
text.Rotherham: He is beclouded, knowing, nothing, rightly, but is diseased about questionings, and word-battles—out of which spring envy, strife, defamations, wicked surmising,
text.KJV: Hee is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions, and strifes of wordes, whereof commeth enuie, strife, railings, euill surmisings,
text.NABRE: is conceited, understanding nothing, and has a morbid disposition for arguments and verbal disputes. From these come envy, rivalry, insults, evil suspicions,
text.DRB: he is proud, knowing nothing, but languishing about questions and strife of words: of which rise envies, contentions, blasphemies, evil suspicions,

text.NRSV: and wrangling among those who are depraved in mind and bereft of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.
text.Rotherham: incessant quarrellings of men wholly corrupt in their mind and bereft of the truth,—supposing godliness to be, a means of gain!
text.KJV: Peruerse disputings of men of corrupt mindes, and destitute of the trueth, supposing that gaine is godlinesse: From such withdraw thy selfe.
text.NABRE: and mutual friction among people with corrupted minds, who are deprived of the truth, supposing religion to be a means of gain.
text.DRB: conflicts of men corrupted in their mind, and that are deprived of the truth, that esteem gain to be piety.

text.NRSV: Of course, there is great gain in godliness combined with contentment;
text.Rotherham: Now it is a great means of gain—godliness, with a sufficiency of one’s own;
text.KJV: But godlinesse with contentment is great gaine.
text.NABRE: Indeed, religion with contentment is a great gain.
text.DRB: But piety with sufficiency is great gain.

text.NRSV: for we brought nothing into the world, so that we can take nothing out of it;
text.Rotherham: For, nothing, brought we into the world, neither, to take anything out, are we able;—
text.KJV: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certaine we can cary nothing out.
text.NABRE: For we brought nothing into the world, just as we shall not be able to take anything out of it.
text.DRB: For we brought nothing into this world, doubtless, neither can we take away any thing.

text.NRSV: but if we have food and clothing, we will be content with these.
text.Rotherham: And, having sustenance and covering, we shall be content;
text.KJV: And hauing food and raiment let vs be therewith content.
text.NABRE: If we have food and clothing, we shall be content with that.
text.DRB: But having food, and wherewith to be covered, with these we are content.

text.NRSV: But those who want to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
text.Rotherham: But, they who are determined to be rich, fall into temptation, and a snare, and many foolish and hurtful covetings, the which, sink men into ruin and destruction,—
text.KJV: But they that will be rich, fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish & hurtfull lusts, which drowne men in destruction and perdition.
text.NABRE: Those who want to be rich are falling into temptation and into a trap and into many foolish and harmful desires, which plunge them into ruin and destruction.
text.DRB: For they that will be made rich, fall into tentation and the snare of the devil, and many desires unprofitable and hurtful, which drown men into destruction and perdition.

text.NRSV: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil, and in their eagerness to be rich some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pains.
text.Rotherham: For, a root of all the vices, is the love of money, which, some, being eager for, have been seduced from the faith, and have pierced, themselves, about with many pangs.
text.KJV: For the loue of money is the root of all euill, which while some coueted after, they haue erred from the faith, and pierced themselues through with many sorrowes.
text.NABRE: For the love of money is the root of all evils, and some people in their desire for it have strayed from the faith and have pierced themselves with many pains. Exhortations to Timothy.
text.DRB: For the root of all evils is covetousness; which certain desiring have erred from the faith, and have entangled themselves in many sorrows.

text.NRSV: But as for you, man of God, shun all this; pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, O man of God! from these things, flee! and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, meekness;
text.KJV: But thou, O man of God, flie these things; and follow after righteousnesse, godlinesse, faith, loue, patience, meekenesse.
text.NABRE: But you, man of God, avoid all this. Instead, pursue righteousness, devotion, faith, love, patience, and gentleness.
text.DRB: But thou, o man of God, fly these things; and pursue justice, piety, faith, charity, patience, mildness.

text.NRSV: Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life, to which you were called and for which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
text.Rotherham: Be contesting the noble contest of the faith,—lay hold of the age-abiding life—unto which thou wast called, and didst make the noble confession before many witnesses.
text.KJV: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternall life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.
text.NABRE: Compete well for the faith. Lay hold of eternal life, to which you were called when you made the noble confession in the presence of many witnesses.
text.DRB: Fight the good fight of faith: apprehend eternal life, wherein thou art called and hast confessed a good confession before many witnesses.

text.NRSV: In the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before Pontius Pilate made the good confession, I charge you
text.Rotherham: I charge thee, before God, who engendereth life in all things, and Christ Jesus, who, before Pontius Pilate, witnessed the noble confession,
text.KJV: I giue thee charge in the sight of God, who quickneth all things, and before Christ Iesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good Confession,
text.NABRE: I charge [you] before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who gave testimony under Pontius Pilate for the noble confession,
text.DRB: I command thee before God who quickeneth all things, and Christ Jesus who gave testimony under Pontius Pilate a good confession,

text.NRSV: to keep the commandment without spot or blame until the manifestation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: That thou keep the commandment without spot, free from reproach, until the forthshining of our Lord Jesus Christ—
text.KJV: That thou keepe this commandement without spot, vnrebukeable, vntill the appearing of our Lord Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: to keep the commandment without stain or reproach until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ
text.DRB: that thou keep the commandment without spot, blameless unto the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: which he will bring about at the right time—he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
text.Rotherham: Which, in its own fit times, the happy and only Potentate will shew—the King of them that reign, and Lord of them that wield lordship,
text.KJV: Which in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed, and onely Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords:
text.NABRE: that the blessed and only ruler will make manifest at the proper time, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
text.DRB: Which in due times the Blessed and only Mighty will shew, the King of kings and Lord of lords,

text.NRSV: It is he alone who has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see; to him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Who alone hath immortality, dwelling in light unapproachable,—Whom no man hath seen—nor can see: unto whom, be honour and might age-abiding. Amen.
text.KJV: Who onely hath immortalitie, dwelling in the light, which no man can approch vnto, whom no man hath seene, nor can see: to whom be honour and power euerlasting. Amen.
text.NABRE: who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, and whom no human being has seen or can see. To him be honor and eternal power. Amen. Right Use of Wealth.
text.DRB: who only hath immortality, and inhabiteth light not accessible, whom no man hath seen, yea neither can see, to whom be honour and empire everlasting. Amen.

text.NRSV: As for those who in the present age are rich, command them not to be haughty, or to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but rather on God, who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
text.Rotherham: Upon them who are rich in the present age, lay thou charge—not to be high-minded, nor to have set their hope on, riches’, uncertainty,—but on God, who offereth us all things richly for enjoying,
text.KJV: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they bee not high minded, nor trust in vncertaine riches, but in the liuing God, who giueth vs richly all things to enioy,
text.NABRE: Tell the rich in the present age not to be proud and not to rely on so uncertain a thing as wealth but rather on God, who richly provides us with all things for our enjoyment.
text.DRB: Command the rich of this world not to be high minded, nor to trust in the uncertainty of riches, but in the living God (who giveth us all things aboundantly to enjoy)

text.NRSV: They are to do good, to be rich in good works, generous, and ready to share,
text.Rotherham: To be doing good, to be rich in noble works, to be, generous in giving, ready for fellowship,—
text.KJV: That they doe good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate,
text.NABRE: Tell them to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous, ready to share,
text.DRB: to do well, to become rich in good works, to give easily, to communicate,

text.NRSV: thus storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of the life that really is life.
text.Rotherham: Treasuring up for themselves a good foundation for the future, that they may lay hold on the life [which is life] indeed.
text.KJV: Laying vp in store for themselues a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay holde on eternall life.
text.NABRE: thus accumulating as treasure a good foundation for the future, so as to win the life that is true life.
text.DRB: to heap unto themselves a good foundation for the time to come, that they may apprehend the true life.

text.NRSV: Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you. Avoid the profane chatter and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge;
text.Rotherham: O Timothy! that which hath been entrusted, do thou guard, avoiding the profane pratings and oppositions of falsely named knowledge,—
text.KJV: O Timothie, keepe that which is committed to thy trust, auoyding prophane and vaine bablings, and oppositions of science, fasly so called:
text.NABRE: VI. Final Recommendation and Warning O Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you. Avoid profane babbling and the absurdities of so-called knowledge.
text.DRB: O Timothee, keep the depositum, avoiding the profane novelties of voices, and oppositions of falsely called knowledge.

text.NRSV: by professing it some have missed the mark as regards the faith. Grace be with you.
text.Rotherham: Which, some professing, concerning the faith, have missed the mark! Favour be with you.
text.KJV: Which some professing, haue erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen. The first to Timothie was written from Laodicea, which is the chiefest citie of Phrygia Pacaciana.
text.NABRE: By professing it, some people have deviated from the faith. Grace be with all of you.
text.DRB: Which certain promising, have erred about the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.
